description: The feather: flight's fabric, the wing's ('wapile') unit, warmth's lightest blanket. What the bird is dressed in and what it leaves behind as a calling card.
sound_symbolism: Breath-light 'phi' — the softest onset the language has — floating through 'le' to the open 'a'. Say it and almost nothing happens: that is the feather.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'philea lue waero lepa.' (A feather falls from the sky.) 'lo philea pelori sulae phelu.' (Feathers hold the bird's warmth.)

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A single feather is a complete composition — spine, barbs, curve — nature's own study in flowing line.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Quill, fletching, stuffing, ornament: the feather was a whole economy shed annually for free.
